---
title: "Command Registry"
---

## Overview

`CommandRegistry` is a singleton that maps command name strings to creator functions
and `CommandInfo` metadata. It replaces the former hardcoded if-else chain in
`CommandFactory.cpp`, enabling domain libraries to contribute commands without
modifying a central switch statement.

**Source files:**

- `src/Commands/Core/CommandRegistry.hpp` — class declaration and `registerTypedCommand<>` helper
- `src/Commands/Core/CommandRegistry.cpp` — singleton implementation
- `src/Commands/Core/register_core_commands.hpp` — declaration of the built-in dispatcher
- `src/Commands/register_core_commands.cpp` — registers all 7 core commands

## Design

The registry follows the **Explicit Dispatcher** pattern (see
[Roadmap Phase 7](roadmap.qmd#phase-7-command-registration-api-explicit-dispatcher)):

1. Each library defines a free function (e.g., `register_core_commands()`) that calls
   `registerTypedCommand<>` for each command it owns.
2. The application's composition root (`MainWindow` constructor) calls each library's
   registration function once at startup.
3. `CommandFactory.cpp` free functions (`isKnownCommandName`, `createCommandFromJson`,
   `getAvailableCommands`, `getCommandInfo`) are thin wrappers delegating to
   `CommandRegistry::instance()`.

This avoids `--whole-archive` linker flags and keeps the dependency direction clean:
domain libraries depend on `Commands`, and the executable depends on both.

## API

### CommandRegistry

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `instance()` | Static accessor for the singleton. |
| `registerCommand(name, creator, info)` | Register a command. Asserts on empty or duplicate name. |
| `isKnown(name)` | Check if a command name is registered. |
| `create(name, params_json)` | Create a command from name + JSON params; returns `nullptr` on failure. |
| `availableCommands()` | Return `vector<CommandInfo>` for all registered commands. |
| `commandInfo(name)` | Return `optional<CommandInfo>` for one command. |
| `size()` | Number of registered commands. |
| `clear()` | Remove all registrations (test isolation only). |

### registerTypedCommand\<CommandT, Params\>

```cpp
template<typename CommandT, typename Params>
void registerTypedCommand(CommandRegistry & reg, std::string name, CommandInfo info);
```

Convenience template that:

1. Extracts `parameter_schema` from `Params` via `extractParameterSchema<Params>()`.
2. Wraps JSON deserialization (`rfl::json::read<Params>`) and `normalizeJsonNumbers` into a
   `CommandCreator` lambda.
3. Calls `reg.registerCommand(...)`.

## Adding a New Command

1. Create the command class (subclass of `ICommand`) and its `Params` struct.
2. In the library's registration function, add one call:

```cpp
registerTypedCommand<MyNewCommand, MyNewCommand::Params>(
    reg, "MyNewCommand",
    CommandInfo{"MyNewCommand", "Category", "Description"});
```

3. If this is a new library that doesn't yet have a registration function, create one
   and call it from `MainWindow` (or a future `register_all_commands.cpp` composition root).

## Test Registration

Test binaries that execute commands (via `createCommandFromJson`, `executeSequence`, etc.)
must call `register_core_commands()` before tests run. A reusable Catch2 event listener
is provided at `tests/shared/RegisterCommandsListener.cpp`. Add it to your test
executable's source list in CMake:

```cmake
add_executable(my_test
    ${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/tests/shared/RegisterCommandsListener.cpp
    # ... test sources ...
)
```

## Thread Safety

Registration is expected to happen once at startup before any concurrent access.
After registration is complete, all query methods (`isKnown`, `create`,
`availableCommands`, `commandInfo`, `size`) are safe for concurrent reads.
